blocks.heading: 1. Scope and Who We Are
blocks.text: This Privacy Policy applies to SWaParty websites, applications, and related API services. By visiting, registering, logging in, or using features such as watch rooms, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this policy.

blocks.heading: 2. Types of Information We Collect
blocks.text:
We may collect the following categories of information:
(1) Account information: email address, hashed password, account status, and display name.
(2) Identity and session information: login session tokens, verification/reset workflow status, and authentication timestamps.
(3) Usage and log information: IP address, browser/device data, access time, error logs, and audit logs.
(4) Media and interaction data: video files and metadata uploaded to your cloud media library, external video links you paste, room chat messages, and playback control events.

blocks.heading: 3. How We Use Information
blocks.text:
We use relevant information to:
(1) Provide core features, including account registration/login, video upload and mounting, link parsing, synchronized playback, and chat interactions.
(2) Maintain security and risk control, including anti-abuse, anomaly detection, account protection, incident troubleshooting, and audit tracking.
(3) Operate and improve the service, including performance analysis, capacity planning, feature optimization, and support.
(4) Fulfill legal obligations, including compliance, investigations, and dispute handling.

blocks.heading: 4. External Platform Link Parsing Notice
blocks.text:
SWaParty supports pasting third-party platform links and parsing playable streams. You understand and agree that:
(1) This feature is provided as a technical relay and playback capability.
(2) You are solely responsible for ensuring your submitted links and playback behavior are lawful and authorized.
(3) We do not guarantee availability, accuracy, or continued accessibility of third-party platform content.

blocks.heading: 5. Sharing and Third-Party Services
blocks.text: To provide the service (including third-party sign-in), we may work with infrastructure and identity partners as necessary (for example, cloud computing, object storage, databases, CDN, email providers, and login platforms). When you sign in with providers such as Google or GitHub, we process only the minimum account identifiers returned by that provider (such as provider user ID, public profile name, email, and verification status) for account creation/linking and security control. We do not obtain your third-party password or access data beyond authorized scope. We do not sell personal information to unrelated third parties, and we disclose it only where legally required or necessary to protect legitimate rights and security.

blocks.heading: 6. Data Location and Cross-Border Transfer
blocks.text: Given our cloud architecture, your data may be stored or processed in server nodes across different regions. We implement reasonable safeguards for security and compliance in cross-border transfers, including least-privilege access control, encrypted transmission, and access auditing.

blocks.heading: 7. Data Retention
blocks.text: We retain data only for the shortest reasonable period necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy. After account deletion, service termination, or a valid deletion request, we will delete or anonymize relevant data subject to legal and security/audit requirements.

blocks.heading: 8. Security Measures
blocks.text: We use common industry safeguards, including transport encryption, password hashing, session controls, access control, audit logs, and least-privilege practices. However, no internet environment is absolutely secure, and you should protect your account credentials and avoid sharing them.

blocks.heading: 9. Your Rights
blocks.text: To the extent permitted by applicable law, you may request access, correction, or deletion of personal information, withdraw consent where applicable, or request account cancellation. You may also manage account security by resetting your password and signing out.

blocks.heading: 10. Minors
blocks.text: If you are a minor, you should use SWaParty with consent and guidance from your parent or legal guardian. Guardians may contact our official support channels regarding minors' personal information matters.

blocks.heading: 11. Policy Updates
blocks.text: We may update this Privacy Policy based on product changes or legal requirements. Updated versions will be published on this page and marked with an effective date. For material changes, we will provide reasonable notice.

blocks.heading: 12. Contact Us
blocks.text: If you have questions, comments, or complaints regarding this Privacy Policy or our personal information practices, please contact us through SWaParty official support channels.

blocks.heading: 1. Acceptance and Applicability
blocks.text: Welcome to SWaParty. By registering, accessing, or using the service, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you must stop using the service immediately.

blocks.heading: 2. Service Scope
blocks.text:
SWaParty provides features including but not limited to:
(1) network video link casting and parsing (including mainstream platform links),
(2) private cloud media library upload, storage, and mounting,
(3) real-time synchronized watch rooms and chat interactions,
(4) account status, storage usage, and related entitlement displays.

blocks.heading: 3. Account Registration and Security Obligations
blocks.text: You must provide true, accurate, and complete registration information and are responsible for all activities under your account. You must safeguard your password and session credentials, and you may not lend, rent, sell, or otherwise provide account access to unauthorized third parties.

blocks.heading: 4. User Content and License
blocks.text: You retain rights to your uploaded content, chat messages, and other user-generated content. To operate the service, you grant us a non-exclusive, revocable license limited to necessary storage, processing, transmission, synchronized display, and security review.

blocks.heading: 5. Intellectual Property and Parsing Compliance
blocks.text: You represent that you only upload, share, or play content you are legally authorized to use. You may not use parsing features to bypass copyright protections, paywalls, or access controls, and you may not engage in infringing distribution. You are solely responsible for disputes, claims, or penalties arising from your content or actions.

blocks.heading: 6. Prohibited Conduct
blocks.text:
You must not:
(1) violate applicable laws, regulations, or platform rules,
(2) distribute illegal, infringing, abusive, hateful, or malicious content,
(3) disrupt system stability, attack APIs, scrape data, or abuse resources,
(4) impersonate others, mass-register accounts, evade risk controls, or spam,
(5) access, copy, or reverse-engineer core service capabilities without authorization.

blocks.heading: 7. Room Order and Host Controls
blocks.text: In synchronized watch rooms, the host has global controls such as play, pause, and seek. You must follow room rules and interact respectfully. For harassment, abuse, spam, or malicious disruption, we may issue warnings, mute users, remove users from rooms, limit features, or suspend/terminate accounts.

blocks.heading: 8. Storage Quotas and Resource Limits
blocks.text: We may set limits for cloud storage, concurrency, bandwidth, file size, and API/request frequency based on plan, promotions, or operational policy. Upload, parsing, playback, or synchronization features may be restricted if limits are exceeded.

blocks.heading: 9. Third-Party Dependencies and Availability Disclaimer
blocks.text: Some SWaParty features depend on third-party platforms and cloud infrastructure, including third-party sign-in services. If interruption, delay, or unavailability (including third-party login failure) results from provider changes, network failures, policy updates, authorization expiration, or force majeure, we will make reasonable efforts to recover service but do not guarantee uninterrupted availability.

blocks.heading: 10. Limitation of Liability
blocks.text: To the maximum extent permitted by law, SWaParty is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. We are not liable beyond legally required scope for indirect damages, loss of expected profits, or data risks arising from your violations, third-party factors, network conditions, or force majeure.

blocks.heading: 11. Breach Handling and Service Termination
blocks.text: If you violate these Terms or applicable law, we may take actions depending on severity, including feature restrictions, content removal, suspension, or termination. You may also request account cancellation under platform rules.

blocks.heading: 12. Updates and Dispute Resolution
blocks.text: We may update these Terms based on business or legal requirements. Updated terms will be posted on this page and become effective upon publication or stated effective date. Disputes should first be resolved through good-faith negotiation; if unresolved, they shall be handled under applicable law by a competent court or arbitration body.
